raw_consumer_statement: I ordered a XXXX XXXX online from XXXX on XX/XX/year> for XXXX dollars. I immediately became suspicious from their vague confirmation email. I tried to cancel order immediately only realize the process was taking me in a circle and i found buried in website cancelations not allowed. Then I researched company and found hundreds if not thousands of people saying they have been ripped off too. I have never received product. I don't want product. I reported to Citi credit card and all they keep saying is merchant wont issue a credit and consumer is responsible. How can a dispute be based off the direction of an obvious criminal and how can a credit card company deny they have to give me my money back? Im looking at Fair Billing XXXX XXXX a XXXX Citibank is not following the law. There is so much proof out there that XXXX is a criminal organization. How is the XXXX government allowing citizens to be stolen from like this? We are supposed to have consumer protection laws for things just like this but credit card company is acting like we dont and that they cant do anything about it.
